heading: Procedure for timely settlement of estates of decedent obligors
label: 1651 Procedure for timely settlement of estates of decedent obligors
text: The Bureau, in consultation with the Bureau and each other agency referred to in , shall prescribe regulations to require any creditor, with respect to any credit card account under an open end consumer credit plan, to establish procedures to ensure that any administrator of an estate of any deceased obligor with respect to such account can resolve outstanding credit balances in a timely manner.1section 1607(a) of this titleSo in original.1
notes: Amendments2010—, substituted “the Bureau” for “the Federal Trade Commission”.Pub. L. 111–203, § 1100A(3), substituted “The Bureau” for “The Board”.Pub. L. 111–203, § 1100A(2)
